text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English, the podcast where we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听《Real Easy English》，这是一档用简单英语进行真实对话、帮助大家学习的播客节目。

text: I'm Neil and with me is Georgie. Hello. Did you know that you can now watch a video of this podcast
zh: 我是尼尔，和我一起的是乔治。大家好。你们知道吗，现在你可以观看这期播客的视频，

text: and you can read along with a transcript on our website? Visit bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 还可以在我们的网站上边看文字稿边听。快去 bbclearningenglish.com 看看吧。

text: Hi Georgie, how are you? I'm very well, thank you, Neil. How are you? How was your journey into work?
zh: 嗨，乔治，你好吗？我很好，谢谢你，尼尔。你呢？你今天上班路上怎么样？

text: My journey into work was very good today, actually, and we're actually talking about transport today.
zh: 其实我今天上班的路上很顺利，我们今天正好要聊聊交通话题。

text: How was your journey to work? My journey was quite easy this morning, actually.
zh: 你上班路上怎么样？说实话，我今天早上的路程挺轻松的。

text: It isn't always. And Neil, we actually have a very similar commute, don't we?
zh: 但也不总是这样。尼尔，其实我们俩的通勤方式挺像的，对吧？

text: I get the Tube to work, which in the UK is the underground train.
zh: 我坐地铁上班，在英国叫“Tube”，也就是地下铁。

text: So I walk to the station around 15 to 20 minutes,
zh: 我先走大约十五到二十分钟到车站，

text: and then I get the underground train, one train, and then I change and then get another one. And then I walk to work.
zh: 然后坐地铁，坐一趟车后要换乘另一趟，再走到公司。

text: What about you? I do that. But before your bit, I have to get a bus as well.
zh: 你呢？我也差不多，不过在你说的那一步之前，我还得先坐公交车。

text: Are you that far away from the station? A couple of miles. Okay.
zh: 你离车站有那么远吗？大概几英里吧。好的。

text: Three kilometres. A bit too far to walk then. A bit too far to walk.
zh: 三公里左右。那走路确实有点远。是啊，太远了走不动。

text: I have walked when the public transport has been unreliable.
zh: 公共交通不靠谱的时候，我也走过路。

text: But normally I get the bus to the tube stop and then I get on the tube into work.
zh: 不过一般我会先坐公交到地铁站，然后再坐地铁去上班。

text: Lovely. And do you like the tube? What do you think about it?
zh: 真好。那你喜欢地铁吗？你觉得它怎么样？

text: I think the tube is very convenient, actually, because the trains go very frequently.
zh: 我觉得地铁其实挺方便的，因为班次很密集。

text: So if you miss one, you can wait just a couple of minutes and get the next one.
zh: 如果错过一趟，只需要等几分钟就能坐下一趟。

text: And it's probably the quickest way to get into central London because there's so much traffic.
zh: 而且它可能是进伦敦市中心最快的方式，因为路上车太多了。

text: Driving is a really bad idea. Yeah, I agree with you.
zh: 开车真的不是个好主意。是啊，我同意你的看法。

text: It is very reliable. it's usually very easy the thing I don't like about it especially in the morning at rush hour
zh: 地铁确实很可靠，通常也很方便。不过我不喜欢的一点，尤其是早高峰的时候，

text: is the number of people on the tube um the the underground trains in London are very old
zh: 就是地铁里的人太多了。伦敦的地铁车厢其实很老旧，

text: they're very small and they are very um crowded
zh: 车厢很小，而且非常拥挤。

text: in the mornings so crowded means there's lots of of people. We can also say packed.
zh: 早上尤其拥挤，“crowded”就是人很多的意思。我们也可以说“packed”。

text: Yeah, and I really don't like being in a tight space with lots of people in the mornings, so early in the morning.
zh: 是啊，我真的很讨厌一大早就被挤在人堆里，那种狭小的空间感。

text: Yeah, I agree. But I'm lucky because I get on the Tube at the first stop, so I always get a seat.
zh: 我也这么觉得。不过我比较幸运，因为我是从始发站上车，所以总能有座位。

text: But then by the time you get on, it is usually really packed.
zh: 可等你上车的时候，通常已经挤满人了。

text: And the closer The closer you get into central London, the more packed it is.
zh: 而且越靠近市中心，就越拥挤。

text: You're squashed up against strangers. It's not always fun.
zh: 你会被挤在陌生人中间，感觉不太好受。

text: I hate it. What about buses? Do you like Buses are okay.
zh: 我特别讨厌这样。那公交车呢？你喜欢坐公交吗？公交车还算可以。

text: In London, again, they are quite frequent and that's convenient.
zh: 在伦敦，公交车班次也挺密集，这一点很方便。

text: Not always. I mean, they are. They're frequent, but they're more unreliable than trains, aren't they?
zh: 但也不总是这样。虽然班次多，但比起火车来说，公交车更不靠谱，对吧？

text: There always seems to be roadworks near where I live.
zh: 我家附近好像总是在修路。

text: And when there are roadworks, there are traffic lights, and then there are delays on the buses.
zh: 一修路就会有红绿灯，然后公交车就容易延误。

text: So in the city, public transport can be great.
zh: 所以说在市区内，公共交通其实可以很棒。

text: But what about when we go outside of the city? What do you use to go to other parts of the UK?
zh: 但如果要去城市以外呢？去英国其他地方你会用什么交通方式？

text: If it's quite far, so if I'm going really far north, maybe to Scotland or to,
zh: 如果距离比较远，比如要去很北边的地方，像苏格兰，或者

text: I don't know, Manchester or a northern city, it's probably better to get the train because it's faster.
zh: 曼彻斯特之类的北方城市，坐火车可能更好，因为更快。

text: And I really like trains. You can relax. It's a relaxing experience.
zh: 而且我特别喜欢坐火车。可以放松身心，是一种很惬意的体验。

text: Some of my best travel experiences have been on long distance trains.
zh: 我有过一些最棒的旅行经历，就是在长途火车上。

text: Yeah. Where have you been that's been so fantastic on a train?
zh: 是吗？你坐火车去过哪儿，觉得特别精彩？

text: Well, I once went from Prague to Moscow on a train.
zh: 我有一次从布拉格坐火车到莫斯科。

text: Wow. Yeah. What was that like? Really exciting. Why?
zh: 哇。是啊。那感觉怎么样？特别刺激。为什么？

text: It took about three days and I was with some friends and we could eat and drink and look out the window.
zh: 那趟车大概花了三天时间，我和几个朋友一起，可以边吃边喝，还能看窗外的风景。

text: The landscape changed frequently. It was just exciting.
zh: 沿途的景色不断变化，非常令人兴奋。

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we heard in this podcast,
zh: 我们来回顾一下这期播客里出现的词汇吧，

text: starting with some useful adjectives to describe public transport.
zh: 先从一些描述公共交通的实用形容词开始。

text: For example, we had crowded, which means very busy. We also hear packed.
zh: 比如我们听到了“crowded”，意思是非常拥挤、繁忙。我们也听到了“packed”。

text: We had reliable, which describes something you can trust, and unreliable, which describes something you can't trust.
zh: 还有“reliable”，形容可以信赖的事物，以及“unreliable”，形容不可靠的事物。

text: We heard frequent, which describes something that happens often.
zh: 我们还听到了“frequent”，形容经常发生的事情。

text: And we also heard delayed, which means something like public transport comes later than expected.
zh: 另外还有“delayed”，意思是公共交通比预期晚到。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 这就是本期《Real Easy English》的全部内容。

text: Don't forget to go to our website where you can get a free worksheet to download to test what you've learned.
zh: 别忘了访问我们的网站，下载免费的练习表，测试一下你学到了多少。

text: It's at bbclearningenglish .com. See you then. Goodbye. Bye.
zh: 网址是 bbclearningenglish.com。我们下次再见，拜拜！
